feb3a79e02bc930b.body: Micro/small MSME lending accelerated 23.77pp in FY26, driving 30.09% YoY growth and capturing 23.12% of total MSME credit. Formalisation via UDYAM + GST makes underwriting scalable, but pure balance-sheet lending cannot match NBFC reach into informal segments. The 20.06pp size-spread compression signals market maturation—early infrastructure builders capture disproportionate origination flow.
feb3a79e02bc930b.implication: For lenders: Build co-lending rails and warehouse-financing partnerships now while NBFC distribution remains fragmented and before competitors lock in the best origination channels.

4a56445ae52c687a.body: Gold loans surged 121.21% year-over-year with banks sustaining 8 consecutive periods of positive momentum, systematically taking market share from NBFCs as gold price appreciation expands secured lending capacity. Banks with existing branch networks hold decisive operational advantages in assay, custody, and cash management that NBFCs cannot replicate at scale. The growth trajectory remains structural—driven by collateral price inflation, not credit speculation—making this a durable revenue expansion opportunity.
4a56445ae52c687a.implication: For lenders: Deploy branch-based gold loan capabilities now while the structural tailwind persists and before NBFC incumbents can build competing operational infrastructure.

010ca380b2d9a684.body: Micro and small MSME credit accelerated 23.77pp in FY2026 to 30.09% YoY growth, now commanding 23.12% of total MSME portfolio share. The size-based growth spread compressed to 20.06pp as smaller borrowers outpaced medium enterprises, validating infrastructure-led origination models. Banks with UDYAM-GST underwriting rails are harvesting first-mover advantage in the formalised micro-borrower segment.
010ca380b2d9a684.implication: For lenders: deploy MSME infrastructure investment within 24 months or accept structural underperformance as the formalisation wave matures and competitive positioning solidifies.

666b500886d66da0.body: PLI capex drawdown is driving exceptional credit demand across anchor sectors—engineering credit surged 34.5% YoY while basic metals and chemicals sustained 17-20% growth through April 2026. These anchor companies are expanding supplier networks to meet PLI production targets, creating validated cash-flow relationships ripe for structured finance.
666b500886d66da0.implication: For lenders: prioritize supply chain finance products targeting tier-1/tier-2 suppliers of PLI recipients over standalone MSME lending—the anchor backstop significantly de-risks the exposure while capturing the sectoral momentum.

111782e8659e3a41.body: PSL housing loans surged 39.94% YoY following the Oct 2024 ceiling revision, creating substantial reclassification surpluses at well-positioned banks. This one-time regulatory windfall generates sellable PSL Certificates for banks exceeding their revised targets. The influx of tradeable certificates deepens PSLC secondary market liquidity for the first time since the ceiling change.
111782e8659e3a41.implication: For lenders: Buy PSLCs from surplus banks at favorable rates while supply peaks, or monetize your own reclassification gains before the market normalizes.

93ce5ef0317a1dc1.body: Infrastructure sub-sector scan shows traditional project finance declining (-26.03%, -44.06%, -11.91%) while renewable segments surge 74.02% year-over-year. The infrastructure supercycle's completion creates a $2T refinancing opportunity as project finance teams pivot from roads and bridges to solar farms and green hydrogen. Mixed signals across infrastructure (21.47% active, 1.41% strengthening, 0.45% stable) confirm the sector rotation underway.
93ce5ef0317a1dc1.implication: For lenders: redeploy existing project finance capabilities to renewable energy — same risk frameworks, higher regulatory tailwinds, deeper government pipeline visibility.

9bef6e4e00965a35.body: Services credit surged 18.58% YoY with 6.99pp FY acceleration, now commanding 28.21% of total credit as NBFC intermediation rebounds (16.68% sub-sector growth, with specialized segments hitting 57.1%). The sector's outperformance across trade, CRE, and professional services creates a bifurcated credit market where services-exposed lenders capture disproportionate growth.
9bef6e4e00965a35.implication: For lenders: Build direct services origination capabilities or establish NBFC partnerships to access the intermediated flow before competitors lock up the channel.

84272ac3d4be9404.body: Credit cards outstanding hit 119.4M in April 2026, growing 8.15% YoY, but PSBs capture only 23.8% share versus their dominant debit card position. Private banks command 71% of the credit card market, creating a 40+ percentage point share gap that PSBs can exploit through targeted debit-to-credit upgrades within their existing customer base. The acquisition economics favor upgrading known customers over cold prospecting in a market adding ~9M new cards annually.
84272ac3d4be9404.implication: For lenders: PSBs should prioritize credit card conversion campaigns targeting their debit card holders, leveraging existing relationship data and transaction history to drive higher-margin product adoption.

395b93576adcd5b5.body: The micro ATM network declined 7.17% YoY to 1.37 million units, signaling sector consolidation as weaker operators exit rural cash distribution. Despite contraction, the 1.37M installed base represents persistent cash demand in markets where full ATM deployment remains uneconomical.
395b93576adcd5b5.implication: For lenders: acquire micro ATM capacity from exiting players to consolidate rural distribution at discounted asset values.

a89fc5166d8d7d11.body: The 'other' credit card category exploded 493% by value and 300% by volume YoY, signaling the emergence of embedded payment infrastructure beyond traditional merchant acceptance. This structural shift — whether transit NFC or RuPay-credit-on-UPI — is creating entirely new card-spend surfaces at platform scale. The growth rates indicate infrastructure rollout, not pilot testing.
a89fc5166d8d7d11.implication: For lenders: identify and integrate with the winning embedded payment platform now, before the land-grab window closes and interchange economics get competed away.

89c7529fe2a1efe4.body: Credit card spending flows are outpacing balance accumulation by 2-6x: transaction volumes grew 21-27% YoY in Apr 2026 while outstanding balances expanded just 3.8% in May 2026. Despite sustained payment growth, credit outstanding remains trapped in single digits for four consecutive periods, confirming the flow→stock lag effect where spending velocity leads balance build-up.
89c7529fe2a1efe4.implication: For lenders: spending strength without balance growth signals creditworthy demand expanding before leverage uptake — the optimal entry point for portfolio growth and risk-adjusted acquisition.
